mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with accuracy below 50%, or interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term equity price adjustments over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.015 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and high FEDFUNDS yields positive forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with accuracy below 52%, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering widespread portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Confirmed recessionary GDP declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 50% of historical instances, or the signal shows no predictive edge with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment signal synchronized supply-side expansion and labor demand, driving positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 5-day returns with accuracy below 50%, or the interaction term loses out-of-sample significance.
